cut.description: First per-cut manifest for the tapes fixture corpus. An unversioned working-tree cut, not yet a tagged release.

provenance_bar: A fixture is admissible when it is synthetic-by-construction: either hand-authored, or a session recorded in a clean-room environment, credential-redacted, and reviewed. No customer content and no live production data.
notes: This manifest is the index for every tapes fixture family. It is deliberately the point of consolidation rather than a single physical directory: the derive corpora (~26 MB) are embedded (go:embed) by the demo seed, and a go:embed directive cannot reference a path above its own package. Homing them next to the seed keeps that 26 MB out of every binary that would import a shared top-level fixtures package. Each family records its physical `location`.
The four-layer pyramid (see README.md): L0 envelope -> L1 wire recordings -> L2 corpus -> L3 rendered API fixtures. Everything is generated downhill from one recording.

families.name: envelope
families.description: Header set -> expected parsed session envelope. Pins the X-Tapes-* header contract that any producer (a proxy injecting the headers) and any consumer (ingest, or another parser) must agree on.

provenance.notes: Hand-authored; no captured content. Header values are produced by a faithful port of the envelope encoding rules and self-checked against their pinned outputs.

consumers: tapes reader oracle: pkg/backfill/envelope_fixtures_test.go runs every case through sessionEnvelopeFromHeaders + Validate
other envelope producers/parsers, via table tests over cases/*.json (vendor this directory to keep one copy)

families.name: recordings
families.description: Verbatim transport bytes for a turn, replayable across every ingest transport for parity checks.
consumers: wire-capture fidelity / replay tests (skipped until bundles land)

families.name: derive-corpus
families.description: Gzipped raw_turns dumps of whole sessions. The deriver is a pure function of these rows, so the reconciliation pipeline regression-tests against them with no database.

provenance.notes: Full live Claude sessions captured through a gateway in a clean-room exercise: the wire envelopes as dispatched by the capture path plus each session's transcript files (main + subagents).

consumers: pkg/seed — demo seed (embeds the two-corpus seed_set only)
downstream client/UI test suites, via tapes dev trace-fixtures
families.embed_note: Physically homed under pkg/seed so the demo seed can go:embed the seed_set (embed can't cross '..'), keeping the 26 MB out of non-seed binaries. corpus-0440f43d is co-located and used by the derive gates and the fixture generator, but deliberately NOT in the seed_set: it is a much larger, many-session capture, and seeding it would triple the embedded payload and change the demo session count.

families.name: capture-reducer
families.description: Provider wire (streaming SSE and one-shot JSON) that drives the reducer tests, including oneshot<->stream pairs that must reduce to the same content hash.

provenance.anthropic: hand-crafted against the Anthropic Messages streaming spec (not recorded traffic); stable placeholder ids (msg_FIXTURE..., toolu_FIXTURE...).
provenance.openai_responses: real Responses wire recorded through a gateway, including the Codex chatgpt stream.

consumers: any external service that embeds the same reducers

families.name: rendered-api
families.location: consuming client/UI repositories (generated, not stored here)
families.description: The session -> traces -> spans projection rendered exactly as the API serves it, for client/UI test suites that want to stay language-independent of the deriver.

provenance.notes: tapes dev trace-fixtures replays the derive corpora through the real deriver and the real API renderers with deterministic session ids.

families.scrub_attestation: derived from the derive-corpus family; no independent capture
families.freshness: Matches projection_schema_version 2026-06-15; regenerate when the deriver or renderers change.
